name: Borlenghe with Lardo and Prosciutto
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large mixing bowl, combine the pasta flour and salt.
2. Add the beaten egg and mix well with your hands or a fork until just combined.
3. Gradually add water, one cup at a time, stirring continuously with a whisk or fork.
4. Stir quickly to incorporate all the water into the dough. The mixture should be very smooth and thin, resembling milk.
5. Set aside for 1 hour to rest. Do not worry if it appears thin; this is normal.
6. In another bowl, combine the lardo, prosciutto, rosemary, garlic, and black pepper until well mixed.
7. Heat a large non-stick skillet over medium heat and brush with 1/4 teaspoon of remaining lardo.
8. Pour enough batter to coat the pan evenly, similar to making a crespella.
9. Cook for 4 minutes on one side, then carefully turn the borlenghe using a spatula.
10. Continue cooking until all the batter is used up and the borlenghe are cooked through, about 10-12 pieces total.
11. To serve, reheat the borlenghe in the same pan for a few seconds to warm them.
12. Spread each piece with the prosciutto mixture, sprinkle with grated Parmigiano-Reggiano, and serve warm, folded into quarters.
Yield: 4 servings
